questions.question_text: Under Act 52 of 2024, real estate wholesalers in Pennsylvania must:
distractors: Register with the local MLS only
Pay an annual wholesaler permit fee
questions.explanation: Act 52 (effective January 9, 2025) expanded RELRA to require wholesalers to obtain a real estate license. The definitions of 'broker' and 'salesperson' were amended to include wholesale transactions.

questions.question_text: A wholesaler enters a contract with a homeowner. Under Act 52, the homeowner may cancel the contract within:
questions.explanation: Act 52 gives sellers the right to cancel a wholesale contract until the sooner of midnight on the 30th day after execution or conveyance of the property. Wholesalers cannot cause sellers to waive this right.

questions.question_text: A wholesaler's contract with a seller fails to include the required Act 52 disclosures. The contract may be:
questions.correct_answer: Canceled at any time before conveyance
distractors: Enforced as written by the buyer
questions.explanation: Agreements that don't comply with Act 52 disclosure requirements may be freely canceled by the seller at any time before the property is conveyed. Non-compliant contracts have no cancellation deadline.

questions.question_text: Which activity requires a Pennsylvania real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: Collecting rents for others for pay
distractors: An owner selling their own home
An attorney drafting a sales contract
An auctioneer at a public auction
questions.explanation: Collecting rents or managing property for others for compensation requires a real estate license. Owners selling their own property, attorneys acting in scope of practice, and auctioneers at public auctions are exempt.

questions.question_text: After a seller cancels a wholesale contract under Act 52, the wholesaler must refund all payments within:
questions.explanation: Under Act 52, when a seller exercises their cancellation right, the wholesaler must refund all payments within 10 business days of the cancellation.

questions.question_text: Which of the following persons is exempt from Pennsylvania real estate licensing requirements?
questions.correct_answer: A court-appointed executor selling estate property
distractors: A friend who receives a referral fee
questions.explanation: Court-appointed fiduciaries (executors, administrators, trustees) are exempt from licensure when acting within their fiduciary role. People receiving referral fees, property managers, and wholesalers all need licenses.

questions.question_text: A licensed attorney negotiates a real estate sale as part of their legal practice. Is a real estate license required?
questions.explanation: Licensed attorneys acting within the scope of their legal practice are exempt from real estate licensing requirements. This exemption covers legal services related to real estate transactions.

questions.question_text: A person acts under a valid power of attorney to sell their neighbor's property. Does this person need a real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: No, acting under power of attorney is exempt
distractors: Yes, unless the seller is present
Only if the person receives compensation
questions.explanation: Persons acting under a valid power of attorney are exempt from licensure requirements in Pennsylvania. This allows designated agents to handle transactions on behalf of absent property owners.

questions.question_text: An auctioneer sells real estate at a private auction for a fee. Does the auctioneer need a real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, the exemption covers public auctions only
distractors: No, all auctioneers are fully exempt
No, if the fee is under $5,000
Yes, but only for commercial property
questions.explanation: The auctioneer exemption in Pennsylvania applies only to public auctions. Selling real estate at a private auction for compensation would require a real estate license.

questions.question_text: A government employee lists a surplus state property for sale as part of their job duties. A real estate license is:
questions.explanation: Government employees acting in their official capacity are exempt from real estate licensing requirements. This covers state and local government personnel disposing of surplus or public property.
